pattern: Each candidate component value must divide the total, so test target sums from small to large and greedily fold subtree sums upward, cutting an edge whenever a subtree exactly reaches the target.
hints: Every component has the same value, so that value must evenly divide the total of all node values, bounding the candidate targets.

For a fixed target, root the tree and let each subtree accumulate its value; when a subtree's running sum hits the target you cut its edge to the parent, and if it ever exceeds the target that target is impossible.
Try the number of components from many down to one (equivalently targets total/parts from small up): the first feasible split maximizes deleted edges, which equals parts minus one.

edge_inputs.description: single node has no edges to cut
total is prime so only the whole tree works
path graph that splits evenly into pairs
uneven values prevent any split despite divisible total

stress.description: random small tree with small values so several targets are feasible to test
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 14)))
    edges = []
    for i in range(1, n):
        p = rng.randint(0, i - 1)
        edges.append([p, i])
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 8) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums, edges]
stress.note: kept small (<=14 nodes) so the brute-force oracle can enumerate every edge-deletion subset
